label: Google Ads
description: Connect to Google Ads to read and manage search and display ad campaigns, budgets, and performance data.

form.title: Connect Google Ads
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Google Ads account using OAuth 2.0 and a developer token.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 + Developer Token
methods.description: Bring your own Google Cloud OAuth client and a Google Ads developer token. Anton opens a browser consent screen, then uses both credentials to make authorized API calls against your ad accounts.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 + Google Ads Developer Token

Google Ads requires **two** separate credentials: an OAuth 2.0 client (from Google Cloud Console) and a **developer token** (from Google Ads Manager Center). Both are mandatory.

### Step 1 — Create an OAuth 2.0 client

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) → pick or create a project.
2. **APIs & Services → Library** → search for and enable the **Google Ads API**.
3. **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen** → configure an internal or external app; add your email as a test user if external.
4. **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID** → choose **Desktop app**.
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** into the fields below.

### Step 2 — Obtain a developer token

1. Sign in to your [Google Ads Manager Account (MCC)](https://ads.google.com/).
2. Click the **Tools & Settings** wrench → **Setup → API Center**.
3. If you haven't applied yet, submit an application. Basic access is granted immediately for testing; standard access requires a short review.
4. Copy the **Developer token** shown on the API Center page and paste it into the field below.

### Step 3 — (Optional) MCC login customer ID

If you manage multiple accounts under an MCC, paste the **manager account customer ID** (10-digit number, no dashes) into **Login customer ID**. Leave blank if you only have a single account.

### Step 4 — Authorize

Click **Submit**. Anton opens Google's consent screen in your browser. Sign in with the Google account that has access to your ad accounts, grant the requested permissions, and Anton stores the resulting refresh token locally.

### Notes

- The `adwords` scope grants broad access to all accounts reachable by the authorized user.
- Developer tokens are shown once per API Center session — bookmark the API Center page for quick retrieval.
- To revoke, visit [Third-party apps with account access](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and remove the entry.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Must be a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; copied from the same row in Cloud Console.

fields.name: developer_token
fields.label: Developer Token
fields.description: Found in Google Ads Manager → Tools & Settings → Setup → API Center. Required for every Google Ads API call.

fields.name: login_customer_id
fields.label: Login Customer ID (MCC)
fields.description: 10-digit manager account (MCC) customer ID — no dashes. Required only if you manage multiple accounts through an MCC. Leave blank for single-account setups.
